docs.title: What is it?
docs.text: thecrbox is a ready-to-use, drop-in hardware platform that converts a regular CR box into a smart air purifier.

docs.title: What is included
docs.text:
• controller board

  • high quality environmental sensor

  • interface board

  • ribbon cable to connect controller and interface boards

docs.title: Status
docs.text:
We are currently in the process of releasing diagrams and basic firmware for thecrbox.

A small test batch of pre-assembled devices is ready to be shipped and will be made available as soon as Wi-Fi provisioning and OTA updates are tested.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• schematics
  • reference firmware
  • this documentation

docs.title: Wi-Fi provisioning
docs.text: thecrbox is in Wi-Fi provisioning mode, allowing it to join your home network, when it is delivered or after a factory reset.

docs.title: Provisioning methods
docs.text:
Instructions vary slightly depending on the device you are using. Please choose from the following options:

  • Android
  • iPhone
  • PC with Wi-Fi
  • laptop with Bluetooth and Chrome browser

Tip: If you have multiple devices available, using an Android device is the easiest method.

docs.title: Welcome!
docs.text: You're here because you're setting up your thecrbox. Great!

docs.title: Why?
docs.text: To access all features (e.g., fine-tuning the PID if needed), thecrbox must be accessible via the Wi-Fi network you plan to use for control. There’s no dedicated Android/iOS app -- you’ll enter your Wi-Fi details during setup.

docs.title: Can it go wrong?
docs.text:
Wi-Fi names and passwords are case-sensitive.

If you enter the wrong credentials and thecrbox does not join your network, you can always force a factory reset and try again.

docs.title: Provisioning methods
docs.text:
Instructions vary slightly depending on the device you are using. Please choose from the following options:

  • Android
  • iPhone
  • PC with Wi-Fi
  • laptop with Bluetooth and Chrome browser

Tip: If you have multiple devices available, using an Android device is the easiest method.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: The new device should be provisioned before it can be used. For more details, see the provisioning instructions.

docs.title: Modes of operation
docs.text:
Once provisioned, the following modes can be used:

Display frame Mode description Fans are offRGBs are off Fan speed is controlled automaticallyRGBs represent AQI level Fan speed is set manuallyRGBs represent AQI level

docs.title: Using the encoder
docs.text:
Most day-to-day interactions with thecrbox happen using the encoder:

⇓ long press ↓ short press ↺ rotate switch mode AUTO / MANUAL / OFF enter / confirm / next change current value

Check the ui map for a complete guide on how to use the encoder.

docs.title: Advanced configuration with the webserver
docs.text: More advanced options (like PID configuration or RGB light scheme) can be configured using the built-in web server. For detailed information, see Web Server Configuration.

docs.title: HomeAssistant integration
docs.text: HomeAssistant integration is available via the ESPHome integration. For detailed setup instructions, refer to the ESPHome Integration guide.

docs.title: ESPHome Integration
docs.text: thecrbox supports integration with Home Assistant via ESPHome. This section outlines the steps to connect your device to your Home Assistant setup and leverage its functionalities.

docs.title: Web Server Configuration
docs.text: This section provides details on configuring thecrbox using its built-in web server. Here you will find instructions on how to access the web interface and manage advanced settings such as PID configuration and RGB light schemes.

docs.title: Android (Improv via BLE)
docs.text:
Your browser doesn’t support Web Bluetooth. Use Chrome/Edge on desktop or Android.

  1. Tap the button above.
  2. Find box2-XXXX in the list of nearby devices.
  3. Click "Pair" and approve the pairing/permission prompt.
  4. Enter your Wi-Fi name and password, then tap OK.

docs.title: Provisioning footer
docs.text: Your device is now ready. We highly recommend starting with the user interface overview, then exploring the UI map for detailed guidance. You can also learn how to use the web server or connect to HomeAssistant.

docs.title: iPhone, Computer, or Android (Manual via Hotspot)
docs.text:
1. In your Wi-Fi settings, join the box2-XXXX network. This network does not have an internet connection; it is for setup only.
  2. In Safari, open http://192.168.4.1 (Note: Use http://, not https://).
  3. Enter your Wi-Fi name and password, then tap Save.

The hotspot will shut down, and your phone should reconnect to your home Wi-Fi network.

docs.title: Provisioning methods
docs.text:
Instructions vary slightly depending on the device you are using. Please choose from the following options:

  • Android
  • iPhone
  • PC with Wi-Fi
  • laptop with Bluetooth and Chrome browser

Tip: If you have multiple devices available, using an Android device is the easiest method.

docs.title: Provisioning with laptop with Bluetooth and Chrome
Laptop with Bluetooth and Chrome Browser (Improv via BLE)
docs.text:
Your browser doesn’t support Web Bluetooth. Use Chrome/Edge on desktop or Android.

  1. Tap the button above.
  2. Find box2-XXXX in the list of nearby devices.
  3. Click "Pair" and approve the pairing/permission prompt.
  4. Enter your Wi-Fi name and password, then tap OK.

docs.title: PC or Any Device with Wi-Fi (Manual via Hotspot)
docs.text:
1. In your Wi-Fi settings, join the box2-XXXX network. This network does not have an internet connection; it is for setup only.
  2. In any browser, open http://192.168.4.1 (Note: Use http://, not https://).
  3. Enter your Wi-Fi name and password, then tap Save.

The hotspot will shut down, and your PC should reconnect to your home Wi-Fi network.

docs.title: Factory reset
docs.text:
• Press and hold the encoder button for 4-10 seconds.

This will reset all non-default settings on thecrbox. This can be useful if incorrect Wi-Fi credentials were provided during initial device configuration, preventing your device from joining the network.

docs.title: Mode selection (AUTO, MANUAL, OFF)
docs.text:
Note

⇓ long press to switch purifier into next mode

Available modes list Step Display frame Mode description Encoder action 1 Fans are offRGBs are off ⇓ long press 2 Fan speed is controlled automaticallyRGBs represent AQI level ⇓ long press 3 Fan speed is set manuallyRGBs represent AQI level ⇓ long press 4 go to step 1

docs.title: AUTO & MANUAL mode: select sensor data
docs.text:
Note

↺ rotate to cycle through available sensors data

AUTO mode: available sensors list Step Display frame Visible sensor / settings Encoder action 1 AQI(=9), AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=20%)RGB brightness(=25%) ↺ rotate 2 PM10(=2µg/m³), PM2.5(=2µg/m³)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=20%)RGB brightness(=25%) ↺ rotate 3 VOC(=53), NOX(=1)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=20%)RGB brightness(=25%) ↺ rotate 4 temperature(=27'C), humidity(=41%)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=20%)RGB brightness(=25%) ↺ rotate 5 IP address of the webserverfan speed(=20%)RGB brightness(=25%) ↺ rotate 6 go to step 1

In addition to the sensor value, the display always shows current fan speed and RGB brightness. In AUTO mode:

  • Fan speed is controlled by the purifier and depends on the pollution level and time of day (e.g., lower max fan speed at night).
  • RGB brightness is controlled by the purifier and depends on the time of day (e.g., lower brightness at night).
MANUAL mode: available sensors list Step Display frame Visible sensor / settings Encoder action 1 AQI(=9), AQI Category(=Great)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=82%)RGB brightness(=100%) ↺ rotate 2 PM10(=2µg/m³), PM2.5(=2µg/m³)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=82%)RGB brightness(=100%) ↺ rotate 3 VOC(=89), NOX(=1)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=82%)RGB brightness(=100%) ↺ rotate 4 temperature(=27'C), humidity(=42%)AQI Category(=Great)fan speed(=82%)RGB brightness(=100%) ↺ rotate 5 IP address of the webserverfan speed(=82%)RGB brightness(=100%) ↺ rotate 6 go to step 1

In addition to the sensor value, the display always shows current fan speed and RGB brightness. In MANUAL mode, these values are selected by the user and will not change automatically.

docs.title: MANUAL mode: set fan speed
docs.text:
Note

↓ short press ↺ rotate to change fan speed

Confirming

After setting the fan speed to the desired value, you can wait or ↓ short press↓ short press to save it immediately.

docs.title: MANUAL mode: set RGB brightness
docs.text:
Note

↓ short press ↓ short press ↺ rotate to change RGB brightness

Confirming

After setting the RGB brightness to the desired value, you can wait or ↓ short pressto save it immediately.

docs.title: AUTO mode: check day/night limits
docs.text:
Note

↓ short press to view current fan speed, rgb brightness limits, and day/night start time
